sections.paragraph:
χάσμα, ατος, τό (χάσκω ‘yawn, gape’; Hes. et al.; Eur.; Hdt. 4, 85; Philo; Jos., Bell. 5, 566, Ant. 6, 27; 7, 242; 2 Km 18:17 χ. μέγα; En 18:11 χ. μέγα) chasm (lit. ‘a yawning’) of the unbridgeable space betw. Abraham and the place of torture Lk 16:26 (Diog. L. 8, 31: acc. to Pythagoras the ψυχαὶ ἀκάθαρτοι cannot approach the ὕψιστος τόπος to which Hermes has brought the ψυχαὶ καθαραί; cp. 1 Esdr 7:10–15; on the topic of finality cp. 2 Cl 8:3; Pind. O. 2, 53–78).—DELG s.v. χαίνω II.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

betw. betw. = between

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

acc. to acc. to = according to

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1081
